title: B2 — Three SKILL.md read fabric-config; delete hardcoded pagination/threshold/pseudo-precision text
description: Add Phase 0.5 'Config Load' chapter to all three skills directing the LLM to Read .fabric/fabric-config.json first. Delete hardcoded `--since="2 months ago"`, `-n 200`, `cap 10/50`, `lookback 8`, `cap 20`, `threshold 10`, `page size 8`, `cap 14` literals — replace with `${config.<key>}` template placeholders. Also delete pseudo-precision phrases like `>60% similarity` (β.2 — vague-precision lint). Mirror to .claude/.codex.

files.target: Phase 0 + git mining commands + cap statements
files.change: Add Phase 0.5 Config Load chapter (Read .fabric/fabric-config.json, fall back to defaults if absent); replace `--since="2 months ago"` → `--since="${config.import_since_months} months ago"`; replace `-n 200` → `-n ${config.import_max_commits}`; replace `cap 10` → `cap ${config.import_cap_archive_candidates}`; replace `cap 50` → `cap ${config.import_cap_review_candidates}`; remove `>60% similarity` phrase + similar pseudo-precision

Add Phase 0.5 Config Load chapter; replace `lookback 8` → `${config.archive_lookback_count}`, `cap 20` → `${config.archive_max_candidates}`, `threshold 10` → `${config.archive_min_threshold}`; remove pseudo-precision
Add Phase 0.5 Config Load chapter; replace `page size 8` → `${config.review_pending_page_size}`, `cap 14` → `${config.review_max_modify_followups}`; remove pseudo-precision

implementation: Draft Phase 0.5 Config Load chapter (canonical template — same wording across all three skills): 'Before Phase 1, Read .fabric/fabric-config.json. If absent or missing fields, use defaults from this table: ...'
In each template, prepend Phase 0.5 immediately after Phase 0 (events.jsonl signal scan)
Use grep to enumerate every hardcoded number (--since, -n, cap, lookback, page size, threshold) and replace with ${config.<key>} reference
Audit each template for vague-precision phrases (`>60%`, `roughly`, `approximately N%`, etc.) and delete or replace with `the configured threshold`
Copy template → .claude → .codex (use install helper or hand-mirror)
Run install-skills-and-hooks.test.ts to verify three-mirror parity

reference.examples: Phase 0 already exists in archive (events.jsonl scan); Phase 0.5 slots in before Phase 1 prep work

criteria: All three template SKILL.md files contain a 'Phase 0.5: Config Load' chapter with consistent wording
`grep -E '(--since="[0-9]+|^-n [0-9]+|cap [0-9]+|lookback [0-9]+|page size [0-9]+|threshold [0-9]+)' packages/cli/templates/skills/*/SKILL.md` returns 0 lines (all hardcodes replaced)
All ${config.X} references match a real fabricConfigSchema field added in TASK-004
`diff -q template mirror` returns 0 lines for all six template↔mirror pairs
install-skills-and-hooks.test.ts passes for all three skills

rationale.chosen_approach: Phase 0.5 prepended + ${config.X} substitution; lets LLM read user config without runtime evaluation
alternatives_considered: MCP tool wrapper that injects config (requires server-side change)
Keep hardcodes, document override via prompt prefix
Inline Read instruction inside each Phase without dedicated chapter
rationale.tradeoffs: LLM must actually Read the config file; failure mode: skill uses defaults — acceptable because defaults match prior hardcodes

manual_checks: Open each template SKILL.md and verify Phase 0.5 Config Load chapter present
Run a skill and verify the LLM actually reads the config (manual trace)
success_metrics: 0 hardcoded pagination/threshold numbers in any template
